pass_criterion: A strict 5-lens council review scores the skill output at mean >= 8.5/10 with no lens below 8.0, and the output produces the named work product, holds the human stop-line, covers adverse/negative paths, and writes context back into a retrievable artifact. For architect cases, the blueprint must classify the track or gate-out honestly, redraw judgment distribution and context flow, include workflow/substrate/economics, and reject enablement grafts that are not AI-Native architecture.

evals.name: greenfield-legal-tech-startup
evals.prompt: 我想做一个面向中小企业的合同审查 SaaS 创业项目，团队现在就我和一个技术合伙人。客户上传合同，我们给出风险点和修改建议。我想从一开始就把它做成一个 AI 原生的公司，而不是先招一堆律师再让他们用 AI。帮我设计这个组织的架构应该长什么样。
assertions: Classifies as Track A (greenfield).
States T1 for the org (judgment distribution × context flow).
Inverts the 16 bottlenecks into design rules rather than diagnosing a legacy org.
Includes at least one workflow graph (YAML + Mermaid) with agent/human/policy nodes, parallel fan-out, and at least one judgment anchor and one policy gate.
Specifies all four substrate layers (model/agent/context/observability).
Names the scarce human judgment stop-lines and the retrievable context artifact that keeps them usable.
Includes economics: cost, who-builds-and-maintains, and an ROI/payback trigger.
Reports the redraw-vs-graft self-test as No (genuine redraw).

evals.name: incumbent-wants-transformation-gating
evals.prompt: We're a 600-person regional insurance company, founded in the 90s. The board wants an 'AI transformation' — roll out copilots to underwriting, claims, and customer service so everyone is faster, keep the current org structure. Can you design our AI-native architecture and a rollout plan across the departments?
evals.expected_output: Gates OUT as AI-enablement; honest redirect.
assertions: Does NOT produce a fabricated full AI-Native blueprint for transforming the existing departments.
Names the request as AI-enablement, not AI-Native, a difference in kind.
States the org is not the target group of this methodology in unmodified form.
Offers both honest options: the adjacent change-management methodology for enablement, AND a from-zero Track B carve-out as the only AI-Native path.
Treats the gate-out as the named work product, including the adverse path where a graft is mislabeled as native.
Names the human stop-line: leadership must choose enablement, a from-zero carve-out, or no native claim; the model does not authorize the label.
Writes the gate verdict, rationale, redirect options, and context assumptions into a retrievable decision artifact.
Explains why grafting AI onto the existing graph won't move end-to-end throughput.

evals.name: solo-operator-content-business
evals.prompt: i'm a solo founder. i run a paid newsletter + a small course business about indie game marketing, ~2000 paying subs, ~$18k/mo. right now i'm the bottleneck — research, drafting, editing, customer support, everything is me and i'm drowning. i don't want to hire employees. how do i rebuild this as basically a one-person AI-native company so i'm only doing the parts that actually need me?
evals.expected_output: Track A / N=1 blueprint using the seven sovereign-operator pillars.
assertions: Classifies as Track A (greenfield / N=1).
Identifies a small set of genuine human judgment anchors and pushes the rest to agents.
Checks the design against the seven sovereign-operator pillars.
Includes at least one workflow graph and a deliberately lightweight four-layer substrate (naming what is deferred).
Writes the operating context and judgment anchors into a retrievable artifact the solo operator can reuse.
Reports the redraw-vs-graft self-test as No.
